heading: Expedited, coordinated environmental review process
label: 47171 Expedited, coordinated environmental review process
notes: References in TextThe National Environmental Policy Act of 1969, referred to in subsecs. Effective DateSection applicable only to fiscal years beginning after , except as otherwise specifically provided, see , set out an Effective Date of 2003 Amendment note under .Sept. 30, 2003section 3 of Pub. L. 108–176section 106 of this title

Categorical Exclusions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–63, title VII, § 788138 Stat. 1314May 16, 2024“(a).—Categorical Exclusion for Projects of Limited Federal AssistanceAn action by the Administrator [of the Federal Aviation Administration] to approve, permit, finance, or otherwise authorize any airport project that is undertaken by the sponsor, owner, or operator of a public-use airport shall be presumed to be covered by a categorical exclusion under FAA [Federal Aviation Administration] Order 1050.1F (or any successor document), if such project—“(1)receives less than $6,000,000 (as adjusted annually by the Administrator to reflect any increases in the Consumer Price Index prepared by the Department of Labor) of Federal funds or funds from charges collected under , United States Code; orsection 40117 of title 49“(2)has a total estimated cost of not more than $35,000,000 (as adjusted annually by the Administrator to reflect any increases in the Consumer Price Index prepared by the Department of Labor) and Federal funds comprising less than 15 percent of the total estimated project cost.“(b).—Categorical Exclusion in EmergenciesAn action by the Administrator to approve, permit, finance, or otherwise authorize an airport project that is undertaken by the sponsor, owner, or operator of a public-use airport shall be presumed to be covered by a categorical exclusion under FAA Order 1050.1F (or any successor document), if such project is—“(1)for the repair or reconstruction of any airport facility, runway, taxiway, or similar structure that is in operation or under construction when damaged by an emergency declared by the Governor of the State with concurrence of the Administrator or for a disaster or emergency declared by the President pursuant to the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 5121“(2)in the same location with the same capacity, dimensions, and design as the original airport facility, runway, taxiway, or similar structure as before the declaration described in this section; and“(3)commenced within a 2-year period beginning on the date of a declaration described in this section.“(c).—Extraordinary CircumstancesThe presumption that an action is covered by a categorical exclusion under subsections (a) and (b) shall not apply if the Administrator determines that extraordinary circumstances exist with respect to such action.“(d).—Rule of ConstructionNothing in this section shall be construed to impact any aviation safety authority of the Administrator.“(e).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Categorical exclusionThe term ‘categorical exclusion’ has the meaning given such term in section 1508.1(d) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations.“(2).—Public-use airport; sponsorThe terms ‘public-use airport’ and ‘sponsor’ have the meanings given such terms in , United States Code.”section 47102 of title 49

Updating Presumed To Conform Limits, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–63, title VII, § 789138 Stat. 1315May 16, 2024“Not later than 24 months after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator [of the Federal Aviation Administration] shall take such actions as are necessary to update the FAA’s [Federal Aviation Administration’s] list of actions that are presumed to conform to a State implementation plan pursuant to section 93.153(f) of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations, to include projects relating to the construction of aircraft hangars.”May 16, 2024

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–176, title III, § 302117 Stat. 2533Dec. 12, 2003“Congress finds that—“(1)airports play a major role in interstate and foreign commerce;“(2)congestion and delays at our Nation’s major airports have a significant negative impact on our Nation’s economy;“(3)airport capacity enhancement projects at congested airports are a national priority and should be constructed on an expedited basis;“(4)airport capacity enhancement projects must include an environmental review process that provides local citizenry an opportunity for consideration of and appropriate action to address environmental concerns; and“(5)the Federal Aviation Administration, airport authorities, communities, and other Federal, State, and local government agencies must work together to develop a plan, set and honor milestones and deadlines, and work to protect the environment while sustaining the economic vitality that will result from the continued growth of aviation.”

Limitations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–176, title III, § 308117 Stat. 2539Dec. 12, 2003“Nothing in this subtitle [subtitle A (§§ 301–309) of title III of , enacting this subchapter, amending sections 40104, 47106, and 47504 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section], including any amendment made by this title [enacting this subchapter and amending sections 40104, 40128, 47106, 47503, and 47504 of this title], shall preempt or interfere with—Pub. L. 108–176“(1)any practice of seeking public comment;“(2)any power, jurisdiction, or authority that a State agency or an airport sponsor has with respect to carrying out an airport capacity enhancement project; and“(3)any obligation to comply with the provisions of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( [4321] et seq.) and the regulations issued by the Council on Environmental Quality to carry out such Act.”42 U.S.C. 4371

Relationship to Other Requirements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–176, title III, § 309117 Stat. 2540Dec. 12, 2003“The coordinated review process required under the amendments made by this subtitle [enacting this subchapter and amending sections 40104, 47106, and 47504 of this title] shall apply to an airport capacity enhancement project at a congested airport whether or not the project is designated by the Secretary of Transportation as a high-priority transportation infrastructure project under Executive Order 13274 [ note] (67 Fed. Reg. 59449; relating to environmental stewardship and transportation infrastructure project reviews).”49 U.S.C. 301

children.text: provides for streamlined coordination among the Federal, regional, State, and local agencies concerned with the preparation of environmental impact statements or environmental assessments under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 4321

provides that all environmental reviews, analyses, opinions, permits, licenses, and approvals that must be issued or made by a Federal agency or airport sponsor for such a project will be conducted concurrently, to the maximum extent practicable; and

provides that any environmental review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval that must be issued or made by a Federal agency or airport sponsor for such a project will be completed within a time period established by the Secretary, in cooperation with the agencies identified under subsection (d) with respect to the project.

children.heading: Aviation Projects Subject to a Streamlined Environmental Review Process.—
children.label: b Aviation Projects Subject to a Streamlined Environmental Review Process.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Any airport capacity enhancement project, terminal development project, or general aviation airport construction or improvement project shall be subject to the coordinated and expedited environmental review process requirements set forth in this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may designate an aviation safety project for priority environmental review.

children.heading: .—Requirements
children.label: B .—Requirements
children.text: A designated project shall be subject to the coordinated and expedited environmental review process requirements set forth in this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish guidelines for the designation of an aviation safety project or aviation security project for priority environmental review.

children.label: I
children.text: the importance or urgency of the project;

children.label: II
children.text: the potential for undertaking the environmental review under existing emergency procedures under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: III
children.text: the need for cooperation and concurrent reviews by other Federal or State agencies; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the prospect for undue delay if the project is not designated for priority review.

children.heading: High Priority of and Agency Participation in Coordinated Reviews.—
children.label: c High Priority of and Agency Participation in Coordinated Reviews.—

children.heading: .—High priority for environmental reviews
children.label: 1 .—High priority for environmental reviews
children.text: Each Federal agency with jurisdiction over an environmental review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval shall accord any such review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval involving a project described or designated under subsection (b) the highest possible priority and conduct the review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval expeditiously.

children.heading: .—Agency participation
children.label: 2 .—Agency participation
children.text: Each Federal agency described in subsection (d) shall formulate and implement administrative, policy, and procedural mechanisms to enable the agency to participate in the coordinated environmental review process under this section and to ensure completion of environmental reviews, analyses, opinions, permits, licenses, and approvals described in subsection (a) in a timely and environmentally responsible manner.

children.heading: .—Identification of Jurisdictional Agencies
children.label: d .—Identification of Jurisdictional Agencies
children.text: With respect to a project described or designated under subsection (b), the Secretary shall identify, as soon as practicable, all Federal and State agencies that may have jurisdiction over environmental-related matters that may be affected by the project or may be required by law to conduct an environmental-related review or analysis of the project or determine whether to issue an environmental-related permit, license, or approval for the project.

children.heading: .—State Authority
children.label: e .—State Authority
children.text: Under a coordinated review process being implemented under this section by the Secretary with respect to a project at an airport within the boundaries of a State, the Governor of the State, consistent with State law, may choose to participate in such process and provide that all State agencies that have jurisdiction over environmental-related matters that may be affected by the project or may be required by law to conduct an environmental-related review or analysis of the project or determine whether to issue an environmental-related permit, license, or approval for the project, be subject to the process.

children.heading: .—Memorandum of Understanding
children.label: f .—Memorandum of Understanding
children.text: The coordinated review process developed under this section may be incorporated into a memorandum of understanding for a project between the Secretary and the heads of other Federal and State agencies identified under subsection (d) with respect to the project and, if applicable, the airport sponsor.

children.heading: Use of Interagency Environmental Impact Statement Teams.—
children.label: g Use of Interagency Environmental Impact Statement Teams.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may utilize an interagency environmental impact statement team to expedite and coordinate the coordinated environmental review process for a project under this section. When utilizing an interagency environmental impact statement team, the Secretary shall invite Federal, State and Tribal agencies with jurisdiction by law, and may invite such agencies with special expertise, to participate on an interagency environmental impact statement team.

children.heading: .—Responsibility of interagency environmental impact statement team
children.label: 2 .—Responsibility of interagency environmental impact statement team
children.text: Under a coordinated environmental review process being implemented under this section, the interagency environmental impact statement team shall assist the Federal Aviation Administration in the preparation of the environmental impact statement. To facilitate timely and efficient environmental review, the team shall agree on agency or Tribal points of contact, protocols for communication among agencies, and deadlines for necessary actions by each individual agency (including the review of environmental analyses, the conduct of required consultation and coordination, and the issuance of environmental opinions, licenses, permits, and approvals). The members of the team may formalize their agreement in a written memorandum.

children.heading: .—Lead Agency Responsibility
children.label: h .—Lead Agency Responsibility
children.text: The Federal Aviation Administration shall be the lead agency for projects described in subsection (b)(1) and shall be responsible for defining the scope and content of the environmental impact statement, consistent with regulations issued by the Council on Environmental Quality. Any other Federal agency or State agency that is participating in a coordinated environmental review process under this section shall give substantial deference, to the extent consistent with applicable law and policy, to the aviation expertise of the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: Effect of Failure To Meet Deadline.—
children.label: i Effect of Failure To Meet Deadline.—

children.heading: .—Notification of congress and ceq
children.label: 1 .—Notification of congress and ceq
children.text: If the Secretary determines that a Federal agency, State agency, or airport sponsor that is participating in a coordinated review process under this section with respect to a project has not met a deadline established under subsection (a)(3) for the project, the Secretary shall notify, within 30 days of the date of such determination, the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives, the Council on Environmental Quality, and the agency or sponsor involved about the failure to meet the deadline.

children.heading: .—Agency report
children.label: 2 .—Agency report
children.text: Not later than 30 days after date of receipt of a notice under paragraph (1), the agency or sponsor involved shall submit a report to the Secretary, the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives, the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, and the Council on Environmental Quality explaining why the agency or sponsor did not meet the deadline and what actions it intends to take to complete or issue the required review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: For any environmental review, analysis, opinion, permit, license, or approval that must be issued or made by a Federal or State agency that is participating in a coordinated review process under this section and that requires an analysis of purpose and need for the project, the agency, notwithstanding any other provision of law, shall be bound by the project purpose and need as defined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the submission of the appropriately completed proposed purpose and need description of the airport sponsor; and

children.label: B
children.text: any appropriately completed proposed revision to a development project that affects the purpose and need description previously prepared or accepted by the Federal Aviation Administration.

children.heading: .—Assistance
children.label: 3 .—Assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide all airport sponsors with technical assistance in drafting purpose and need statements and necessary supporting documentation for projects involving Federal approvals from more than 1 Federal agency.
consider only those alternatives to the project that the Secretary has determined are reasonable; and

children.label: A
children.text: subject matter areas within the special expertise of the agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: changes necessary to ensure the agency is carrying out the obligations of that agency under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and other applicable law.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Solicitation and Consideration of Comments
children.label: l .—Solicitation and Consideration of Comments
children.text: In applying subsections (j) and (k), the Secretary shall solicit and consider comments from interested persons and governmental entities in accordance with the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and , Code of Federal Regulations.42 U.S.C. 4321section 1503 of title 40

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of publication of a notice of intent to prepare an environmental impact statement or the initiation of an environmental assessment, the Secretary of Transportation shall establish a plan for coordinating public and agency participation in and comment on the environmental review process for a project described or designated under subsection (b). The coordination plan may be incorporated into a memorandum of understanding.

children.heading: .—Cloud-based, interactive digital platforms
children.label: B .—Cloud-based, interactive digital platforms
children.text: The Secretary is encouraged to utilize cloud-based, interactive digital platforms to meet community engagement and agency coordination requirements under subparagraph (A).

children.label: I
children.text: interim milestones and deadlines for agency activities necessary to complete the environmental review; and

children.label: II
children.text: completion of the environmental review process for the project.

children.label: I
children.text: the responsibilities of participating agencies under applicable laws;

children.label: II
children.text: resources available to the cooperating agencies;

children.label: III
children.text: overall size and complexity of the project;

children.label: IV
children.text: the overall time required by an agency to conduct an environmental review and make decisions under applicable Federal law relating to a project (including the issuance or denial of a permit or license) and the cost of the project; and

children.label: V
children.text: the sensitivity of the natural and historic resources that could be affected by the project.

children.heading: .—Maximum project schedule
children.label: iii .—Maximum project schedule
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable and consistent with applicable Federal law, the Secretary shall develop, in concurrence with the project sponsor, a maximum schedule for the project described or designated under subsection (b) that is not more than 2 years for the completion of the environmental review process for such projects, as measured from, as applicable, the date of publication of a notice of intent to prepare an environmental impact statement to the record of decision.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: I .—In general
children.text: Any issue or dispute that arises between the Secretary and participating agencies (or amongst participating agencies) during the environmental review process shall be addressed expeditiously to avoid delay.

children.label: aa
children.text: implement the requirements of this section consistent with any dispute resolution process established in an applicable law, regulation, or legally binding agreement to the maximum extent permitted by law; and

children.label: bb
children.text: seek to resolve issues or disputes at the earliest possible time at the project level through agency employees who have day-to-day involvement in the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: aa .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make information available to each cooperating and participating agency and project sponsor as early as practicable in the environmental review regarding the environmental, historic, and socioeconomic resources located within the project area and the general locations of the alternatives under consideration.

children.heading: .—Sources of information
children.label: bb .—Sources of information
children.text: The information described in item (aa) may be based on existing data sources, including geographic information systems mapping.

children.label: aa
children.text: identify, as early as practicable, any issues of concern regarding any potential environmental impacts of the project, including any issues that could substantially delay or prevent an agency from completing any environmental review or authorization required for the project; and

children.label: bb
children.text: communicate any issues described in item (aa) to the project sponsor.

children.heading: .—Elevation for missed milestone
children.label: V .—Elevation for missed milestone
children.text: If a dispute between the Secretary and participating agencies (or amongst participating agencies) causes a milestone to be missed or extended, or the Secretary anticipates that a permitting timetable milestone will be missed or will need to be extended, the dispute shall be elevated to an official designated by the relevant agency for resolution. The elevation of a dispute shall take place as soon as practicable after the Secretary becomes aware of the dispute or potential missed milestone.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: VI .—Exception
children.text: Disputes that do not impact the ability of an agency to meet a milestone may be elevated as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Further evaluation
children.label: VII .—Further evaluation
children.text: If a resolution has not been reached at the end of the 30-day period after a relevant milestone date or extension date after a dispute has been elevated to the designated official, the relevant agencies shall elevate the dispute to senior agency leadership for resolution.

children.heading: .—Consistency with other time periods
children.label: D .—Consistency with other time periods
children.text: A schedule under subparagraph (C) shall be consistent with any other relevant time periods established under Federal law.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the Secretary may lengthen or shorten a schedule established under subparagraph (C) for good cause. The Secretary may consider a decision by the project sponsor to change, modify, expand, or reduce the scope of a project as good cause for purposes of this clause.

children.heading: .—Lengthened schedule
children.label: I .—Lengthened schedule
children.text: The Secretary may lengthen a schedule under clause (i) for a cooperating Federal agency by not more than 1 year after the latest deadline established for the project described or designated under subsection (b) by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Shortened schedule
children.label: II .—Shortened schedule
children.text: The Secretary may not shorten a schedule under clause (i) if doing so would impair the ability of a cooperating Federal agency to conduct necessary analyses or otherwise carry out relevant obligations of the Federal agency for the project.

children.label: i
children.text: the cooperating Federal agency shall, not later than 10 days after failing to meet the deadline, submit to the Secretary a report that describes the reasons why the deadline was not met; and

children.label: I
children.text: submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a copy of the report under clause (i); and

children.label: II
children.text: make the report under clause (i) publicly available on a website of the Department of Transportation.

children.label: i
children.text: provided to all participating agencies and to the State department of transportation of the State in which the project is located (and, if the State is not the project sponsor, to the project sponsor); and

children.label: ii
children.text: made available to the public.

children.label: i
children.text: a different deadline is established by agreement of the lead agency, the project sponsor, and all participating agencies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the deadline is extended by the lead agency for good cause.

children.label: i
children.text: a different deadline is established by agreement of the Secretary, the project sponsor, and all participating agencies; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the deadline is extended by the lead agency for good cause.

children.label: A
children.text: as soon as practicable after the 180-day period, an initial notice of the failure of the Federal agency to make the decision; and

children.label: B
children.text: every 60 days thereafter until such date as all decisions of the Federal agency relating to the project have been made by the Federal agency, an additional notice that describes the number of decisions of the Federal agency that remain outstanding as of the date of the additional notice.

children.heading: .—Involvement of the public
children.label: 4 .—Involvement of the public
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall reduce any time period provided for public comment in the environmental review process under existing Federal law, including a regulation.

children.heading: Concurrent Reviews and Single NEPA Document.—
children.label: n Concurrent Reviews and Single NEPA Document.—
A
children.text: carry out the obligations of such agency under other applicable law concurrently, and in conjunction, with the review required under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), unless doing so would impair the ability of such agency to conduct needed analysis or otherwise carry out such obligations; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: formulate and implement administrative, policy, and procedural mechanisms to enable the agency to ensure completion of the environmental review process in a timely, coordinated, and environmentally responsible manner.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable and consistent with Federal law, all Federal permits and reviews for a project shall rely on a single environmental document prepared under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) under the leadership of the Secretary.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Secretary shall develop an environmental document sufficient to satisfy the requirements for any Federal approval or other Federal action required for the project, including permits issued by other Federal agencies.

children.heading: .—Cooperation of participating agencies
children.label: ii .—Cooperation of participating agencies
children.text: In carrying out this subparagraph, other participating agencies shall cooperate with the lead agency and provide timely information.

children.heading: .—Treatment as participating and cooperating agencies
children.label: C .—Treatment as participating and cooperating agencies
children.text: A Federal agency required to make an approval or take an action for a project, as described in this paragraph, shall work with the Secretary to ensure that the agency making the approval or taking the action is treated as being both a participating and cooperating agency for the project.

children.label: i
children.text: the project sponsor requests that agencies issue separate environmental documents;

children.label: ii
children.text: the obligations of a cooperating agency or participating agency under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) have already been satisfied with respect to the project; or42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary determines that reliance on a single environmental document (as described in subparagraph (A)) would not facilitate timely completion of the environmental review process for the project.

children.label: A
children.text: provide comments, responses, studies, or methodologies on areas within the special expertise or jurisdiction of the agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: use the process to address any environmental issues of concern to the agency.

children.label: A
children.text: cite the sources, authorities, and reasons that support the position of the agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: if appropriate, indicate the circumstances that would trigger agency reappraisal or further response.

children.label: A
children.text: the final environmental impact statement or record of decision makes substantial changes to the project that are relevant to environmental or safety concerns; or

children.label: B
children.text: there is a significant new circumstance or information relevant to environmental concerns that bears on the proposed action or the environmental impacts of the proposed action.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an environmental impact statement shall not exceed 150 pages, not including any citations or appendices.

children.heading: .—Extraordinary complexity
children.label: B .—Extraordinary complexity
children.text: An environmental impact statement for a proposed agency action of extraordinary complexity shall not exceed 300 pages, not including any citations or appendices.

children.heading: Integration of Planning and Environmental Review.—
children.label: p Integration of Planning and Environmental Review.—
A
children.text: The lead agency for a project, with respect to an environmental impact statement, environmental assessment, categorical exclusion, or other document prepared under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: B
children.text: A cooperating agency with responsibility under Federal law with respect to the process for and completion of any environmental permit, approval, review, or study required for a project under any Federal law other than the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), if consistent with such Act.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Identification
children.label: 2 .—Identification
children.text: If a lead or cooperating agency makes a determination to adopt or incorporate by reference and use a planning product under paragraph (1), such agency shall identify the agencies that participated in the development of the planning products.

children.heading: .—Adoption or incorporation by reference of planning products
children.label: 3 .—Adoption or incorporation by reference of planning products
A
children.text: adopt or incorporate by reference an entire planning product under paragraph (1); or

children.label: B
children.text: select portions of a planning project under paragraph (1) for adoption or incorporation by reference.

children.label: A
children.text: be made at the time the lead and cooperating agencies decide the appropriate scope of environmental review for the project; or

children.label: B
children.text: occur later in the environmental review process, as appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: The planning product was developed through a planning process conducted pursuant to applicable Federal law.

children.label: B
children.text: The planning product was developed in consultation with appropriate Federal and State resource agencies and Indian Tribes.

children.label: C
children.text: The planning process included broad multidisciplinary consideration of systems-level or corridor-wide transportation needs and potential effects, including effects on the human and natural environment.

children.label: D
children.text: The planning process included public notice that the planning products produced in the planning process may be adopted during any subsequent environmental review process in accordance with this section.

children.label: i
children.text: made the planning documents available for public review and comment by members of the general public and Federal, State, local, and Tribal governments that may have an interest in the proposed project;

children.label: ii
children.text: provided notice of the intention of the such agency to adopt or incorporate by reference the planning product; and

children.label: iii
children.text: considered any resulting comments.

children.label: F
children.text: There is no significant new information or new circumstance that has a reasonable likelihood of affecting the continued validity or appropriateness of the planning product or portions thereof.

children.label: G
children.text: The planning product has a rational basis and is based on reliable and reasonably current data and reasonable and scientifically acceptable methodologies.

children.label: H
children.text: The planning product is documented in sufficient detail to support the decision or the results of the analysis and to meet requirements for use of the information in the environmental review process.

children.label: I
children.text: The planning product is appropriate for adoption or incorporation by reference and use in the environmental review process for the project and is incorporated in accordance with, and is sufficient to meet the requirements of, the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) and section 1502.21 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Effect of adoption or incorporation by reference
children.label: 6 .—Effect of adoption or incorporation by reference
A
children.text: incorporated directly into an environmental review process document or other environmental document; and

children.label: B
children.text: relied on and used by other Federal agencies in carrying out reviews of the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a process to track, and annually submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives, the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate, the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate a report on projects described in subsection (b)(1) that contains the information described in paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: begins on the date on which a notice of intent is published in the Federal Register; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ends on the date on which the Secretary issues a record of decision, including, if necessary, a revised record of decision; and

children.label: i
children.text: begins on the date on which the Secretary makes a determination to prepare an environmental assessment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ends on the date on which the Secretary issues a finding of no significant impact or determines that preparation of an environmental impact statement is necessary.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of proposed actions for which a categorical exclusion was applied by the Secretary during the reporting period;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of proposed actions for which a documented categorical exclusion was applied by the Secretary during the reporting period;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of proposed actions pending on the date on which the report is submitted for which the issuance of a documented categorical exclusion by the Secretary is pending;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of proposed actions for which an environmental assessment was issued by the Secretary during the reporting period;

children.label: E
children.text: the length of time the Administration took to complete each environmental assessment described in subparagraph (D);

children.label: F
children.text: the number of proposed actions pending on the date on which the report is submitted for which an environmental assessment is being drafted by the Secretary;

children.label: G
children.text: the number of proposed actions for which a final environmental impact statement was completed by the Secretary during the reporting period;

children.label: H
children.text: the length of time that the Secretary took to complete each environmental impact statement described in subparagraph (G);

children.label: I
children.text: the number of proposed actions pending on the date on which the report is submitted for which an environmental impact statement is being drafted; and

children.label: i
children.text: project funding has been identified; and

children.label: ii
children.text: all other Federal, State, and local activities that are required to allow the proposed action to proceed are completed.

children.heading: .—Environmental assessment
children.label: A .—Environmental assessment
children.text: The term “environmental assessment” has the meaning given such term in section 1508.1 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation).

children.heading: .—Environmental impact statement
children.label: B .—Environmental impact statement
children.text: The term “environmental impact statement” means a detailed statement required under section 102(2)(C) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ().42 U.S.C. 4332(2)(C)

children.heading: .—NEPA process
children.label: C .—NEPA process
children.text: The term “NEPA process” means the entirety of the development and documentation of the analysis required under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), including the assessment and analysis of any impacts, alternatives, and mitigation of a proposed action, and any interagency participation and public involvement required to be carried out before the Secretary undertakes a proposed action.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Proposed action
children.label: D .—Proposed action
children.text: The term “proposed action” means an action (within the meaning of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.)) under this title that the Secretary proposes to carry out.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Reporting period
children.label: E .—Reporting period
children.text: The term “reporting period” means the fiscal year prior to the fiscal year in which a report is issued under subsection (a).
